review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-099-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.description: Denied state has secondary_interaction === primary_interaction (both 'inspect_permission_badge') because safeActions.at(-1) of a single-element array equals safeActions[0]. The scope calls out primary/secondary interactions; the denied state has no distinct secondary.

schema_version bumped v0.5->v0.6 and the prior current_pack (CP00-098) was relocated to a new ui_state_pack key while current_pack becomes CP00-099. Only the in-repo validator is updated to read ui_state_pack; any external consumer pinning v0.5 or reading current_pack as the UI-state pack would need updating. Low risk, no consumer in diff.

original_review_findings.description: Denied-state focus/keyboard inconsistency. actions=['inspect_permission_badge'] but focus_order is hardcoded to ['inspect_permission_badge','open_review_reference','inspect_audit_hint'], so the tab order and the attached audit_hint focus stop reference two controls (open_review_reference, inspect_audit_hint) that are intentionally absent from the denied action set. The coverage validator further mandates focus_order.includes('inspect_audit_hint') for EVERY state, locking the mismatch in: a denied focus order trimmed to match its actions would fail validation. Keyboard/focus behavior does not match the available denied interactions.

New surface_id 'permission_audit_binding_panel' is not registered in CORE_DOMAIN_UI_SURFACES within this staged diff, yet every state is built via createCoreDomainUiState(surface_id, ...). Correct construction (and that base does not throw on an unregistered surface) is not verifiable from the staged diff. If base validates surface membership strictly, the entire CP00-099 matrix/coverage/tests would throw and this escalates.

State name 'review_required' diverges from the upstream UI-state contract's 'review' state. Tests assert denied.permission_badge.effect==='deny' and review.permission_badge.effect==='review', but effect is inherited from base.permission_badge (createCoreDomainUiState, outside this diff) which is not shown to map the new 'review_required' name to 'review'. Same for layout.min_touch_target_px/max_columns and permission_badge.effect — all base-derived and unverifiable from the staged diff.

Denied state has secondary_interaction === primary_interaction (both 'inspect_permission_badge') because safeActions.at(-1) of a single-element array equals safeActions[0]. The scope calls out primary/secondary interactions; the denied state has no distinct secondary.

schema_version bumped v0.5->v0.6 and the prior current_pack (CP00-098) was relocated to a new ui_state_pack key while current_pack becomes CP00-099. Only the in-repo validator is updated to read ui_state_pack; any external consumer pinning v0.5 or reading current_pack as the UI-state pack would need updating. Low risk, no consumer in diff.

notes: No-write / Risk-A boundaries are solidly enforced and consistent across all three surfaces of truth: contract JSON, JS contract, state objects, coverage validator, and the contract-validation script all assert evaluates_runtime_permission=false, writes_audit_event=false, writes_product_state=false, creates_database_rows=false, accepts_real_client_data=false, synthetic_only=true, uses_real_client_data=false, ldip_implemented=false; permission_badge is reference_only_never_evaluated with evaluated=false and approval_implied=false; audit_hint is display_only_never_written with writes_audit_event=false and ledger_event_created=false; forbidden_claims block runtime_permission_evaluated/audit_event_written/product_state_mutated/real_client_data_loaded/ldip_implemented. Denied and review_required states, primary/secondary interactions, error copy (both messages avoid implying approval or a performed write; review copy attributes any future record to a separate 'audit kernel'), responsive desktop_dense/mobile_single_column matrix (4 cells), keyboard policy (badge-before-audit-hint), evidence packet, read-only Claude prompt, and CP00-100/RP01.P04.M05.S15 handoff are all present and internally consistent at the JSON<->JS<->validator level. No P0/P1: no runtime permission evaluation, no audit ledger writes, no product-state writes, no real data, no LDIP. The P2 items are correctness/consistency nits in synthetic contract metadata plus cross-file dependencies on the unchanged base helpers createCoreDomainUiState/freezeUiState (surface registration, state-name->effect mapping, layout fields) that cannot be confirmed from the staged diff alone — they are flagged as caveats, not boundary violations. Recommend either trimming denied focus_order to its available actions (and relaxing the validator's blanket inspect_audit_hint requirement for denied) or documenting that the badge/audit-hint focus stops are always-rendered display references; and reconciling the 'review_required' vs upstream 'review' naming.
